risk_rationale: Establishes the node-ID-to-file-offset addressing convention (nodeID * NodeSize, node ID 0 reserved) that 1.2.3 (insert/split), 1.2.4 (delete), and 1.2.5 (prefix scan) will all build on -- getting this wrong or inconsistent would ripple through the rest of the epic.
Establishes the internal-node child-descent traversal rule as executable code (not just doc prose) for the first time -- future subtasks reuse this exact rule.
Introduces a test-only tree-building helper that must be unambiguously marked as scaffolding, not the real insert API, to avoid confusion with 1.2.3's actual deliverable.

downstream_dependents.depends_on: NodeStore, traversal rule, Lookup for verifying post-delete state
NodeStore, NextLeaf-based leaf chaining, traversal rule to find scan start leaf

public_api_additions: func OpenNodeStore / NewNodeStore(f *os.File) *NodeStore (exact name TBD in plan)
func Lookup(store *NodeStore, rootNodeID uint64, path string) (fileID uint64, found bool, err error)

concurrency_scope.note: Lookup and NodeStore in this subtask are single-threaded / no locking, consistent with docs/LLD/btree.md deferring latch-crabbing and optimistic-read version-check/retry to a later concurrency-focused subtask. Version field is read/round-tripped (already on the structs) but not used for any CAS/retry logic here.

test_scaffolding_risk.risk: A hand-built, non-splitting tree-construction helper could be mistaken for a real insert implementation by a future reader or by /cdr:verify.
test_scaffolding_risk.mitigation: Helper lives only in lookup_test.go (not exported, not in lookup.go), named buildTestTree, with an explicit doc comment stating it is test-only scaffolding and is NOT subtask 1.2.3's insert API.

rollback_plan: Single self-contained commit adding two new files; revertable with a single git revert without touching node.go or any other package.
